message.content:
# 1. Package-Page Outline: `node-fetch` (npm)

---

## Overview

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Package** | `node-fetch` |
| **Ecosystem** | npm |
| **Latest published version** | 3.3.2 (per npm registry metadata) |
| **Approximate weekly downloads** | ~130.6 million (week of 2026-04-08 to 2026-04-14, per npm API) |
| **Known published advisories** | 3 (per OSV and GitHub Advisory database) |

---

## Known Vulnerabilities

### 1. Credential Leakage on Redirect — GHSA-r683-j2x4-v87g / CVE-2022-0235

- **Bug class:** Redirect credential leakage (authorization/cookie headers forwarded to untrusted origins during HTTP redirects)
- **Severity:** Per GitHub Advisory record; classified as a security-sensitive header exposure issue.
- **Affected versions:** Versions prior to 2.6.7 (2.x line) and prior to 3.1.1 (3.x line).
- **Fixed in:** 2.6.7, 3.1.1
- **Public references:** GitHub Advisory GHSA-r683-j2x4-v87g; NVD CVE-2022-0235; upstream commits/PRs cited in advisory.

### 2. Redirect Bypass of `size` Option (Resource Exhaustion / DoS) — GHSA-w7rc-rwvf-8q5r / CVE-2020-15168

- **Bug class:** Response size limit bypass on redirect, enabling potential denial-of-service through uncontrolled resource consumption.
- **Severity:** Per GitHub Advisory record; classified as a denial-of-service vector.
- **Affected versions:** Versions prior to 2.6.1 (2.x line) and prior to 3.0.0-beta.9 (3.x line).
- **Fixed in:** 2.6.1, 3.0.0-beta.9
- **Public references:** GitHub Advisory GHSA-w7rc-rwvf-8q5r; NVD CVE-2020-15168; upstream commits/PRs cited in advisory.

### 3. Inefficient Regular Expression Complexity (ReDoS) — GHSA-vp56-6g26-6827 / CVE-2022-2596

- **Bug class:** Regular expression denial-of-service (ReDoS) via crafted input triggering catastrophic backtracking.
- **Severity:** Per GitHub Advisory record; classified as a denial-of-service vector.
- **Affected versions:** Versions prior to 3.2.10 (3.x line).
- **Fixed in:** 3.2.10
- **Public references:** GitHub Advisory GHSA-vp56-6g26-6827; NVD CVE-2022-2596; v3.2.10 release tag.

---

## Security Posture Notes

- All three known published advisories have corresponding fixes available in released versions. The current latest version (3.3.2) post-dates all three fix versions and therefore, based on the advisory version-range data, is not within the affected ranges of any of the three listed advisories.
- The three vulnerabilities span two distinct denial-of-service sub-classes (resource exhaustion via redirect size-limit bypass; ReDoS) and one confidentiality issue (credential/header leakage on cross-origin redirect). These are separate bug classes and should not be conflated.
- Weekly download volume (~130.6M) indicates extremely wide adoption, meaning any residual use of unpatched versions carries broad ecosystem risk.

---

## Recommendations

1. **Consumers** should verify they are running node-fetch ≥ 3.2.10 (3.x line) or ≥ 2.6.7 (2.x line) to be beyond the patched versions for all three known advisories.
2. **Dependency auditors** should flag any transitive dependency pins below the fix versions listed above.
3. **No additional action** is indicated by the currently gathered evidence for users on version 3.3.2.

---

## Open Questions

- **2.x maintenance status:** It is not confirmed from the gathered evidence whether the 2.x release line continues to receive security patches beyond 2.6.7. Consumers still on 2.x should verify upstream maintenance posture.
- **ReDoS fix scope in 2.x:** The advisory for GHSA-vp56-6g26-6827 lists a fix only in 3.2.10. It has not been confirmed from the gathered evidence whether the 2.x line was affected or separately patched for the ReDoS issue.
- **Additional unreported issues:** Only publicly disclosed advisories with OSV/GHSA records were reviewed. This does not preclude the existence of undisclosed or not-yet-published vulnerabilities.

---

# 2. Review-Log Entry

**2026-04
